summary.summary: Finds the shortest path between two blocks using breadth-first search. Returns an array of block IDs representing the path from source to destination, or null if no path exists.
summary.docSummary: The shortestPath function computes the shortest path between two blocks in a graph. It uses breadth-first search to find the minimal sequence of blocks connecting the source to the destination, making it suitable for routing and reachability analysis.

summary.summary: Clears the last entry tick cache to reset signal state for all blocks
summary.docSummary: Resets all signal tracking state. Call it to initialize or clear the entry history for block signals.

summary.summary: Determines whether a train can enter a block based on minimum headway enforcement between consecutive entries
summary.docSummary: Validates whether a train is allowed to enter a block, enforcing the minimum headway constraint between consecutive entries.

summary.summary: Records the tick timestamp when a train enters a block for subsequent headway constraint checking
summary.docSummary: Logs the entry time of a train into a block. Used by the signal system to enforce minimum headway between consecutive trains.

summary.summary: Executes a train dispatcher simulation over discrete time ticks, managing train dispatching from a timetable, block occupancy, interlocking constraints, and dwell times. Returns a simulation report documenting all train movements and state changes.
summary.docSummary: The runSchedule function simulates railway operations by processing a timetable and executing train movements across a block-based network. It manages block occupancy, interlocking and signal constraints, dispatches trains according to departure times, handles station dwell periods, and records all movements via a MovementRecorder. Use this as the main entry point to run a complete railway simulat

summary.summary: Resets the interlocking system by clearing all block holdings and section reservations, restoring it to an initial state.
summary.docSummary: Clears all block occupancy and single-track reservations, restoring the interlocking system to an initial state. Call this to reset the system between sessions or for testing.

summary.summary: Checks whether a block is available for occupation, returning true if unoccupied or already held by the requesting train.
summary.docSummary: Determines if a block can be occupied by a train. Returns true if the block is unoccupied or already reserved by the requesting train, enabling safe block allocation decisions.

summary.summary: Registers a block as occupied by a train for interlocking control and conflict prevention.
summary.docSummary: Marks a block as occupied by a train. Called when a train enters a block to track occupancy and prevent simultaneous access.

summary.summary: Releases a block from train occupation, freeing it for use by other trains.
summary.docSummary: Removes a block from occupancy tracking. Called when a train exits a block to restore availability for other trains.

summary.summary: Reserves exclusive access to single-track section blocks (bS1, bS2) for a train, preventing conflicts on critical sections. Returns false if another train holds the reservation.
summary.docSummary: Reserves single-track sections (bS1, bS2) for exclusive train use. Returns true if the reservation succeeds or the block is non-critical; returns false if another train already holds the section.

summary.summary: Releases all single-track section reservations held by a train, freeing critical sections for other trains.
summary.docSummary: Clears all single-track section reservations for a specific train. Call when a train exits single-track sections to restore availability.

summary.summary: Formats a train timetable into a column-aligned string representation with headers, train IDs, classes, routes, and departure times.
summary.docSummary: Generates a human-readable timetable displaying train schedules with ID, class, route, and departure information in aligned columns for easy reference.

summary.summary: Transforms a simulation report into a formatted text summary displaying metrics, on-time rate, and per-train arrival status.
summary.docSummary: Generates a formatted text report from simulation results, displaying completion ticks, on-time rate, and arrival status for each train, indicating whether it arrived on schedule or was stranded.

summary.summary: Comparator function for sorting queue entries by train ID in reverse (descending) order for priority-based queue operations.
summary.docSummary: Comparator function for use with Array.sort() to order QueueEntry objects by train ID in descending order. Prioritizes entries with higher train IDs. Intended for queue ordering in priority-based systems.

summary.summary: Computes the proportion of scheduled trains with at least one on-time arrival (within scheduledArrival + ON_TIME_SLACK tolerance), used as a service quality KPI.
summary.docSummary: Key performance indicator that measures train service punctuality by calculating what percentage of scheduled trains achieved on-time arrival status. A train is considered on-time if it has at least one arrival record within the scheduled time plus ON_TIME_SLACK tolerance.

summary.summary: Returns a constant traversal time value regardless of input block. Always returns T, ignoring the block parameter.
summary.docSummary: Retrieves the traversal time for a block. Currently returns a fixed constant value independent of the specific block.

summary.summary: Searches the BLOCK_INFO array for a block matching the given ID, returning the block information or undefined if not found.
summary.docSummary: Retrieves metadata for a specific block by its ID. Returns the matching BlockInfo record or undefined if the block ID does not exist in the network.

summary.summary: Returns an array of block IDs directly adjacent to the given block by filtering the ADJACENCY list and extracting target blocks.
summary.docSummary: Retrieves all blocks directly adjacent to a given block in the network graph. Returns an array of block IDs that can be reached in a single traversal step.
